title: The One With The Fake Monica

expressions.dialogue:
Joey: How could someone get a hold of your credit card number?
Monica: I have no idea. But look how much they spent!
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.nuance: "obtain"보다 구어적. 정보나 사람에게 어렵게 접근하는 맥락 모두에 씀. "get hold of"로도 쓰임. 부정적인 방법으로 얻는 상황에도 자연스럽게 쓰임.
expressions.example: I've been trying to get a hold of him all day, but he won't pick up. / 하루 종일 그에게 연락하려 했는데 전화를 안 받더라.

expressions.dialogue:
Monica: It's just such reckless spending.
Ross: I think when someone steals your credit card, they've kind of already thrown caution to the wind.
expressions.speaker: Ross
expressions.nuance: 위험을 알면서도 개의치 않고 과감히 행동할 때 씀. 가벼운 농담조에서 진지한 비판까지 폭넓게 사용. "kind of"를 붙이면 Ross처럼 약간 아이러니한 뉘앙스.
expressions.example: She threw caution to the wind and quit her stable job to travel the world. / 그녀는 조심성을 내던지고 안정적인 직장을 그만두고 세계 여행을 떠났다.

expressions.dialogue:
Joey: Stalin...Stalin...do I know that name? It sounds familiar.
Chandler: Well, it does not ring a bell with me...
Joey: (Writes it down) Joe Stalin. Y'know, that's pretty good.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 어디서 들어본 것 같다, 귀에 익다
expressions.nuance: 주로 부정문("doesn't ring a bell") 또는 의문문("Does that ring a bell?")으로 씀. 기억날 듯 말 듯한 느낌. Chandler가 독재자 이름을 모르는 척하는 농담에서 아이러니하게 사용됨.
expressions.example: Does the name "Green Park Café" ring a bell? I think we went there together. / "그린파크 카페"라는 이름 기억 안 나? 같이 간 것 같은데.

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Perhaps because you gave her an engagement ring? Y'know Ross doctors are supposed to be smart.

occ.speaker: Hayley
occ.text: no it's not ringing any bells.

expressions.dialogue: Rachel: Okay Monica, y'know what, honey, you're kinda losing it here! I mean, this is really becoming like a weird obsession thing.
expressions.speaker: Rachel
expressions.meaning: 제정신을 잃어가다, 이성·감정을 잃고 집착하거나 무너지다
expressions.nuance: "losing it"은 감정·이성을 잃는다는 뜻. "kinda"를 붙이면 살짝 부드러워지지만 걱정·비판의 뉘앙스는 유지됨. 친한 사이에서만 쓰는 직접적인 표현.
expressions.example: You haven't slept in three days over this project. I think you're kinda losing it. / 이 프로젝트 때문에 3일 동안 못 잔 거잖아. 좀 이상해지고 있는 것 같아.

occ.speaker: Chip
occ.text: Ehh, y'know after high school, you just kinda lose touch. Oh yeah! I ran into Richard Dorfman.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Hey, y'know Ross, I think I kinda understand why I kinda lost it today.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Okay, okay, okay, look, just don't freak out, but I kinda lost it. I know it's in the apartment, but I definitely lost it.

expressions.dialogue:
Rachel: Okay Monica, y'know what, honey, you're kinda losing it here! I mean, this is really becoming like a weird obsession thing.
Phoebe: This is madness. It's madness, I tell you, for the love of God, Monica, don't do it!!
expressions.speaker: Rachel
expressions.nuance: "obsession"에 "thing"을 붙이면 격식을 낮추고 구어적으로 만듦. "it's becoming a thing"처럼 "thing"을 덧붙여 어떤 패턴·현상을 가볍게 이름 붙이는 미국 구어 습관.
expressions.example: He checks his phone every five minutes. It's starting to become a real obsession thing. / 그가 5분마다 핸드폰을 확인해. 진짜 집착 같은 게 되어가고 있어.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Yeah, at Macy's. You were the Obsession girl, right? I was the Aramis guy. (pretends to spray cologne) Aramis? Aramis?

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Okay, Pheebs, y'know what, let's look at this objectively all right? Ninth grade, right? The obsession starts. All right? The summer after ninth grade he sees me in a two-piece for the first time, his obsession begins to grow. So then...

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Okay, Pheebs, y'know what, let's look at this objectively all right? Ninth grade, right? The obsession starts. All right? The summer after ninth grade he sees me in a two-piece for the first time, his obsession begins to grow. So then...

expressions.dialogue: Phoebe: This is madness. It's madness, I tell you, for the love of God, Monica, don't do it!! ...Thank you.
expressions.speaker: Phoebe
expressions.nuance: 종교적 표현이지만 신앙과 무관하게 감정이 극도로 고조될 때 씀. Phoebe처럼 유머러스하게 과장하거나, 진심 어린 절박함을 표현할 때 모두 사용.
expressions.example: For the love of God, can you please stop tapping your pen during the meeting? / 제발, 회의 중에 펜 두드리는 것 좀 멈춰줄 수 있어?

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Well, we're, we're just goin' over here so that we can get away from the horrible flesh eating virus, for the love of God woman, listen to me. Is he lookin', is he lookin'?

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Leave me alone! For the love of God, leave me alone!!! (Joey hangs up.)

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Oh for the love of God!

expressions.dialogue:
Monica: Okay, I'm not getting this!
Phoebe: (Dancing enthusiastically) I'm totally getting it!
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.meaning: 이게 안 된다, 지금 이걸 이해/습득 못 하겠다
expressions.nuance: "I don't understand"보다 현장감 있는 구어 표현. 지금 이 순간 실시간으로 못 따라가고 있다는 느낌. "I don't get it"(일반적 이해)과 달리 "I'm not getting this"는 현재진행 중인 학습·활동에서 씀.
expressions.example: Can you explain it again? I'm not getting this part of the formula. / 다시 설명해줄 수 있어? 이 공식 부분이 안 잡혀.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: I'm not even getting married! Okay, this is a question for science fiction writers!

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: I'm not getting into this again!

expressions.dialogue:
Teacher: Well that's all right, you can come up to the front and dance with me.
Monica: Why don't I just take off my clothes and have a nightmare.
(After Phoebe picks Rachel as partner)
Monica: Great. It's gym class all over again.
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.meaning: (불쾌한 과거 경험이) 또 다시 반복되는 것 같다
expressions.nuance: 주로 과거의 안 좋은 기억이 재현될 때 씀. "It's [과거 상황] all over again" 형태로 트라우마·실망을 환기. 단순 반복("again")보다 감정적인 무게가 훨씬 강함.
expressions.example: The new manager micromanages everyone. It's my old job all over again. / 새 매니저가 모든 걸 일일이 간섭해. 예전 직장이 또 반복되는 것 같아.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: That is a wonder. So tell me something, Mom. If you had to do it all over again, I mean, if she was here right now, would you tell her?

occ.speaker: Barry
occ.text: (to Mindy) Sweetheart, just gimme- gimme another chance, okay, we'll start all over again. We'll go back to Aruba.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: I can't believe you're dressing up for him. I mean, you're just, you're setting yourself up all over again.

expressions.dialogue: Ross: Naturally our first choice would be one of the bigger state zoos, y'know, like, uh, San Diego... right? But that might just be a pipe dream, because, y'know, he's out of state.
expressions.speaker: Ross
expressions.nuance: 아편 흡연 중 꾸는 환각에서 유래한 표현. 현실적으로 이루기 어려운 바람이나 계획에 씀. 자조적이거나 포기에 가까운 뉘앙스. "just a pipe dream"처럼 쓰면 더 체념한 느낌.
expressions.example: Opening a restaurant in Paris sounds amazing, but right now it's just a pipe dream. / 파리에서 식당을 여는 건 멋지게 들리지만, 지금으로선 그냥 공상이야.

expressions.dialogue: Dr. Baldhara: Well, it's technically not a zoo per se, it's more of an interactive wildlife experience.
expressions.speaker: Dr. Baldhara
expressions.nuance: 라틴어에서 온 표현으로 앞서 한 말을 살짝 수정하거나 제한할 때 씀. 완전히 부정하지 않고 범주를 조정하는 뉘앙스. 구어와 문어 모두 자연스럽게 쓰임.
expressions.example: It's not illegal per se, but it's definitely against company policy. / 엄밀히 불법은 아니지만, 분명히 회사 규정에는 어긋나.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Perfect. Fasten your seatbelts, it's peepee time. (She goes into Joey and Chandler's apartment, where Mr. Tribbiani is reading the paper) Hey, Mr. Trib.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Oh, nononowaitwaitwaitnono! Uh... we may not know anything about radiators per se, but we do have a certain amount of expertise in the heating and cooling... mileu.

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: (on phone) Yeah, hi, I was just beeped. (pause) No, Andr?is not here. (to Joey) Third time today. (on phone) Yes, I'm sure... No, sir. I don't perform those kind of services.

expressions.dialogue: Dr. Baldhara: You're making a big mistake here. I mean, San Diego's all well and good, but if you give him to me, I'll start him off against a blind rabbit and give you twenty percent of the gains.
expressions.speaker: Dr. Baldhara
expressions.nuance: 앞의 내용을 인정하면서 반론이나 단점을 제시하는 전형적인 양보 구문. 항상 "but" 뒤에 반박이 옴. 상대방 선택을 존중하는 척하면서 설득할 때 효과적.
expressions.example: A promotion is all well and good, but have you considered the extra hours it comes with? / 승진은 좋은데, 그에 따르는 초과 근무는 생각해봤어?

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: All right well let's see, Ross is a good father, but Joey has a boat-This is hard!

expressions.dialogue:
Ross: She says as time goes on, he's gonna start getting aggressive and violent.
Chandler: So what does this mean?
Ross: I'm gonna have to give him up.
expressions.speaker: Ross
expressions.nuance: "give up on"(희망을 포기)과 달리 "give ~ up"은 실제로 무언가를 다른 곳에 보내거나 포기하는 물리적 행위. 감정적 무게가 있는 표현. 중독 끊기(give up smoking)에도 씀.
expressions.example: She had to give her dog up when she moved into a no-pets apartment. / 그녀는 반려동물 금지 아파트로 이사하면서 강아지를 떠나보내야 했다.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Did he give you that whole "You're-not-up-to-this" thing again?

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Oh, you're... (gives up)

occ.speaker: Phoe
occ.text: Look, I, y'know, I don't mind taking it slow, I like him a lot, y'know he's really interesting and he's really sweet and why won't he give it up?

expressions.dialogue:
Ross: Marcel, no! Good boy. See, how can nobody want him?
Rachel: Oh, somebody will.
(Marcel is clearly scratching and damaging the chair)
Phoebe: I'd say that chair's taking the brunt.
expressions.speaker: Phoebe
expressions.nuance: "bear the brunt"로도 씀. 재해, 비판, 스트레스의 가장 직접적인 피해를 받는 대상을 가리킴. Phoebe처럼 유머러스한 맥락에도, 심각한 상황에도 모두 쓸 수 있음.
expressions.example: The coastal towns took the brunt of the typhoon while inland areas were mostly unaffected. / 해안 마을들이 태풍의 직격탄을 맞은 반면 내륙은 대부분 피해를 입지 않았다.

expressions.dialogue:
Monica: Nope. Going to the Big Apple Circus today.
Rachel: Okay Monica, what are you doing? You're gonna lose your job! ... the restaurant called, they wanna know if you're gonna be showing up for work?
expressions.speaker: Rachel
expressions.nuance: "attend"보다 캐주얼. 나타날지 불확실한 상황에서 특히 씀. "show up for"는 의무나 약속이 걸린 자리에 나타나는 것. 노쇼(no-show) 위기를 암시하는 맥락에서 자주 등장.
expressions.example: He didn't show up for the team meeting, and no one could reach him. / 그가 팀 회의에 나타나지 않았고 아무도 연락이 안 됐다.

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: 'That thing'? This is how you greet guests at a party? Let me ask you something, if I showed up here with my new girlfriend, she wouldn't be welcome in your home?

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Oh, uh, I... don't... care. (Joey's date shows up) Ok, now, remember, no trading. You get the pretty one, I get the mess.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: I'm hoping that when Bob doesn't show up, she will seek comfort in the open arms of the wry stranger at the next table.

expressions.dialogue:
Ross: She says Marcel's humping thing's not a phase. Apparently he's reached sexual maturity.
Joey: (To Chandler) Hey! He beat ya.
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.nuance: "beat you to it"은 누가 먼저 어떤 일을 해버렸을 때 씀. "ya"는 "you"의 구어체. Joey의 경우 Chandler의 미성숙함을 원숭이와 비교하는 블랙코미디 농담.
expressions.example: I was going to suggest the same solution, but she beat me to it. / 나도 같은 해결책을 제안하려 했는데 그녀가 먼저 말해버렸어.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: (to Ross): Ahhh, that's alright. Y'know, that's a tough hand to beat.

occ.speaker: Caroline
occ.text: I think my uterus just skipped a beat.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: I can't believe this, just like 2 weeks ago I was watching Sudden Death, now I'm on a date with Jean-Claude Van Damme. Can you beat up that guy? [he nods] Can you beat up that guy?

expressions.dialogue:
Monica: Ross, you've got to do something about the humping.
Ross: What? It's, it's just a phase.
Chandler: Well, that's what we said about Joey...
Ross: Would you all relax? It's not that big a deal.
expressions.speaker: Ross
expressions.meaning: 그렇게 대단한 일이 아니다, 별거 아니다
expressions.nuance: "It's not a big deal"의 강조형. "that"을 넣으면 "그 정도로"라는 뉘앙스가 강해짐. 상대방의 과민반응을 진정시키거나 사안을 축소할 때 씀. 실제로 대수롭지 않을 때도, 회피할 때도 모두 쓸 수 있음.
expressions.example: So you sent the email to the wrong person. It's not that big a deal — just apologize and resend it. / 이메일을 잘못된 사람한테 보낸 거잖아. 그게 그렇게 대단한 일이야? 사과하고 다시 보내면 되지.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Would you let it go? It's not that big a deal.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Alright, come on you guys, it's not that big a deal. Really... I mean, I just go down there every other day and... make my contribution to the project. Hey, hey, but at the end of two weeks, I get seven hundred dollars.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Yeah, uh, it's actually not that big a deal.

expressions.dialogue:
Monica: I know. It's just such reckless spending.
Ross: I think when someone steals your credit card, they've kind of already thrown caution to the wind.
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.nuance: "careless"(부주의)보다 강하고 결과를 의식적으로 무시하는 뉘앙스. "reckless driving", "reckless spending", "reckless behavior" 등 다양한 조합으로 쓰임. 법적·도덕적 비판에도 자주 등장.
expressions.example: His reckless spending during the holidays left him drowning in debt by January. / 연휴 동안의 무분별한 소비 때문에 1월이 되자마자 빚더미에 올랐다.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Really? Just casually strewn about in that reckless haphazard manner?

expressions.dialogue:
Monica: Oh really? Okay, let's compare, shall we.
Rachel: (Yawning) Oh, it's so late for 'Shall we'...
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.nuance: "let's"보다 다소 격식적이거나 아이러니한 뉘앙스. Rachel의 반응처럼 상황에 어울리지 않는 딱딱한 분위기를 만들 수 있음. 영국식 영어에서 더 일반적이나, 미국 영어에서는 약간 과장되거나 드라마틱한 느낌으로 씀.
expressions.example: It's getting late. Shall we head out before the traffic gets worse? / 늦어지고 있네. 차 막히기 전에 나갈까?

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Ha, ha, very funny, very funny. But I think we'd like to give poker another try. Shall we, ladies?

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Alright, shall we?

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Maybe that's because you haven't taken the time to get to know him. Let's remedy that, shall we?

expressions.dialogue:
Rachel: Are you drunk?!
Monica: Noooo! (Comes closer and whispers) I'm lying. I am so drunk.
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.meaning: 방금 한 말이 거짓말이에요 (유머러스한 즉각 자백)
expressions.nuance: 부정한 직후 바로 뒤집는 패턴은 영어 구어에서 흔한 유머 장치. "just kidding"과 비슷하지만 더 솔직하고 자기 폭로적. 속삭이듯 말하거나 사이를 두고 말하면 코믹 효과 극대화.
expressions.example: A: "Did you eat the last piece of cake?" B: "No, absolutely not." (pause) "...I'm lying. It was incredible." / A: "케이크 마지막 조각 네가 먹었어?" B: "아니, 절대." (잠시 후) "...거짓말이야. 진짜 맛있었어."

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: No, no, no. I'm sorry. It's "smelly cat, smel-ly cat".

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: I'll make something up! I'm good at lying, I actually did borrow your Walkman!

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: No, he's right, I'm totally lying.

expressions.dialogue:
Rachel: Where the hell've you been?
Monica: Monica and I just crashed an embassy party.
Rachel: Are you drunk?!
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.nuance: "party crasher"라는 명사형도 있음. 불법은 아니지만 대담하고 뻔뻔한 행동. 구어에서 유쾌한 모험의 뉘앙스로 자주 쓰임. Fake Monica의 영향을 받은 Monica가 새로운 자아를 실험하는 장면에서 등장.
expressions.example: We didn't have invitations, so we just crashed the gallery opening and pretended to be art collectors. / 초대장이 없어서 갤러리 오프닝에 그냥 몰래 들어가서 미술 수집가인 척했어.

occ.speaker: Mrs. Bing
occ.text: No. Because I know how to write men that women fall in love with. Believe me, I cannot sell a Paolo. People will not turn three hundred twenty-five pages for a Paolo. C'mon, the guy's a secondary character, a, y'know, complication you eventually kill off.

occ.speaker: Malcom
occ.text: (reading) I met Phoebe today. She was really nice to me eventhough I'm such a loser. And, then when I was walking home I thought about her a lot, it was weird, but kinda cool.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Yeah, either that, or Gloria Estefan was right, eventually, the rhythm is going to get you.

expressions.dialogue: Fake Monica: I thought that movie was so incredibly... boring. I mean, that thing at the end where the kid kills himself because he can't be in the play? What was that?! It's like, kid, wait a year, leave home, do some community theatre.
expressions.speaker: Fake Monica
expressions.meaning: 저게 뭐야?! / 그게 뭔 짓이야?! (황당함·어이없음을 표현)
expressions.nuance: 누군가의 행동이나 결정이 황당하거나 이해가 안 될 때 씀. "What were you thinking?!"과 비슷하지만 더 직접적. 사람의 행동뿐 아니라 영화·책의 장면을 비판할 때도 씀.
expressions.example: He just walked out in the middle of the presentation. What was that?! / 발표 도중에 그냥 나가버렸어. 그게 뭔 짓이야?!

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: What? There was ice there that night with Carol? Plastic seats? Four thousand angry Pittsburgh fans?

occ.speaker: Director
occ.text: I think his butt would like to get this shot before lunch. Once again, rolling... water working... and action....and cut. What was that?

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Oh, then no thanks. What the hell was that? Mental note: If Jill Goodacre offers you gum, you take it. If she offers you mangled animal carcass, you take it.

expressions.dialogue:
Fake Monica: You're gonna go back to being exactly who you were, because that's who you are.
Monica: Not necessarily...
Fake Monica: Yes necessarily!
expressions.speaker: Monica / Fake Monica
expressions.meaning: 반드시 그런 건 아니다, 꼭 그렇지는 않다
expressions.nuance: 상대방 주장을 완전히 부정하지 않고 가능성을 열어두는 부드러운 반박. "No"보다 덜 대립적. Fake Monica의 "Yes necessarily!"는 문법적으로 틀린 유머이지만, 강조를 위해 구어에서 종종 쓰임.
expressions.example: A: "Does studying more hours always mean better grades?" B: "Not necessarily — how you study matters more." / A: "공부 시간이 많을수록 성적이 좋아지는 건가요?" B: "꼭 그런 건 아니에요 — 공부 방법이 더 중요해요."

expressions.dialogue:
Monica: She buys clothes from stores that I'm intimidated by the sales people. She spent three hundred dollars on art supplies.
Rachel: You're not an artist.
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.nuance: "scared"보다 사회적 압박이나 위계감에서 오는 두려움. 고급 매장 점원, 유명인, 상사, 권위 있는 사람 앞에서 위축될 때 자주 씀. 자신이 격이 낮다고 느끼는 심리를 솔직하게 표현하는 단어.
expressions.example: I've always been a little intimidated by people who seem to have everything figured out. / 모든 걸 다 알고 있는 것처럼 보이는 사람들 앞에서 항상 조금 위축됐어.

occ.speaker: Julie
occ.text: I know, I probably shouldn't even tell you this, but I'm pretty much totally intimidated by you.

occ.speaker: Charlie
occ.text: Ok, you want the dirt? Alby was seriously insecure. I mean, he was really intimidated by the guy I dated before him.

expressions.dialogue:
Joey: I dunno what to say, Ross. Uh, it's a monkey.
Ross: Just, just say what you feel.
Joey: Marcel, I'm hungry.
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.nuance: "I don't know what to say"의 구어체 단축형. 감동적이거나 어색한 이별·감사의 순간에 솔직한 당혹감을 표현. Joey의 경우 진심으로 할 말이 없다는 블랙코미디가 이 표현을 극대화함.
expressions.example: I dunno what to say — this is genuinely the kindest thing anyone has ever done for me. / 뭐라고 해야 할지 모르겠어 — 이건 누군가 나한테 해준 것 중 가장 친절한 일이야.

expressions.dialogue:
Joey: Isn't there any way you can keep him?
Ross: No, no. The vet says unless he's in a place where he has regular access to some... monkey lovin,' he's just gonna get vicious. I've just gotta get him into a zoo.
expressions.speaker: Joey / Ross
expressions.nuance: "There has got to be"의 구어 단축형. "There must be"보다 더 캐주얼하고 감정이 실림. 해결책을 간절히 찾거나 상황을 믿기 어려울 때 씀. 미국 구어에서 매우 자주 쓰이는 패턴.
expressions.example: There's gotta be a better way to handle this than starting all over. / 처음부터 다시 시작하는 것보다 이걸 처리할 더 나은 방법이 분명히 있을 거야.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: C'mon, you're going out with the guy! There's gotta be something wrong with him!

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Maybe they do. I've been doin' this ten years and I haven't gotten anywhere. There's gotta be a reason.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: (on tv) Oh, you said it Mike. (rips open the carton and spills milk on the counter) Aw! There's got to be a better way!
